title: Restarting the Journey
subtitle: Embracing the new changes.
author: Rudi Prihandoko
content:
The Internet was different back then. When I say "back then," I mean of course the early 2000s. When I was young and free. Hey, opened up my old blogs and still found some guide to how to install pidgin and connect it with Yahoo Messenger. Do the gen alpha even know what Yahoo Messenger is? Gosh. Getting older make me feel more sentimental. 
 
 I started to registered my blog after I was ini junior high. I don't even remember what it was. MySpace was one of the things back then. But of course I couldn't find a trace of my blog since it also closed down a while ago. The oldest one that I could trace back is wordpress and blogspot. Wordpress is still going strong nowadays. Blogspot? They changed after Google bought it. I think it was before YouTube bought by Google, or around the same time. I don't even remember. I just know that I had a blogspot blog and then it was gone. 
 
 My last attemp before this one is using github pages and Jekyll. The template is pretty good, pretty neat and I think I really like it even now. But I stucked and didn't write anything. So, I abandoned it too. Oh there is one with Notion too. But I think Notion gave me lots of distraction since it has really nice UI/UX but also has tons of tools. That Notion also abandoned, but not because of the Notion itself but because I was busy in real life. Or so I thought. 
 
 Fast forward today, I think I want to start again. Stuck in that thesis because I haven't write for a while is so frustating. lol. Last night, I picked up Claude and ask me to make me a blog template, lol. It wasn't as good as that Jekyll template, but at least it decent. Hence this post. 
 
 Cheers, 
 🍻
